text: 21g of fat. saturated fat is a major pressure point. for meats, fat quality matters a lot once the protein is already there.
0g of carbs. everything else here is pretty flat. in this category, carbs barely matter.
25g of protein. bioavailability and amino acid quality are both strong. strong protein is expected here, so the rest of the profile decides how high it climbs.
vitamin b12 at 88% daily value stands out most. that is major vitamin support.
zinc at 30% daily value, iron at 11% daily value stand out most. useful, but still not a major mineral edge.

the upsides first: complete protein delivery is reliable. the category is supposed to do this well. high satiety protein source. that is a major real-world strength for meat. easy to centre a meal around. practical meal value matters.

the drawbacks next: fat quality can go sideways fast. some meats are strong until the fat profile shows up. quality swings a lot by cut and processing. the category can look very different depending on what version you actually buy. sourcing quality can change the real-world result a lot. not every version of the same meat is equal.
B tier. Best when you want efficient protein and can accept the category tradeoffs that come with it.
